sutta: 100. now the exalted 1 was 1 staying near kosambi in gosita park and at that time kakuda akolian the venerable maha mogulanas attendant had just died and was reborn in a mind-created body and the form that he took was such that it filled 2 or 3 magadan village fields yet that form caused suffering neither to him nor to another and kakuda deva visited the venerable maha mogalana saluted him and stood at 1 side and so standing he spoke thus to the vulnerable 1 sir in devadatta has arisen this longing it is i who will lead the order of monks and sir with the arising of that thought devadatta's psychic power has declined thus spoke the deva kakuda and having spoken so he saluted the venerable maha mogalana and keeping him on his right disappeared depends then when the verbal maha mughlana to the exalted 1 and saluting him sat down at 1 side and he told the exalted 1 all that had occurred and the exalted and the exalted 1 said 1 mughallander have you with your mind so compassed the mind of kakuda deva as to know whatsoever kakuda deva says all that is just thus and not otherwise lord i have so compassed his mind so as to know that the mind of kaku so as to know on that whatevs whatsoever kakuda deva says all that is just thus and not otherwise then the buddha said then watch your words mawlana watch your words for even now the foolish fellow will betray himself these are 5 teachers found in the world what 5 take the case mawlana of some teacher whose ways are impure but he thinks i am pure pure are my ways clean and stainless but his disciples know this worthy teacher is impure in his ways but thinks just otherwise if we tell the householders he will not like it and how can we do what he will not like and he is honored by gifts of the requisites the robes arms lodging and medicines what the self shall do even by that shall the self be known the disciples protect such a teacher in his ways and such a teacher expects this protection of his disciples again some teachers mode of livelihood is not pure all his dharma teaching is not real or his exposition all his knowledge and insight are not pure and his disciples know the disciples protect such a teacher and such a teacher expects this protection of his disciples these moghalana are 5 features found in the world but i mughlana and pure in ways and know that i am pure i know that my ways are pure clean and stainless my disciples protect not my ways nor do i expect this protection of my disciples i am pure in my mode of livelihood in dhamma teaching in exposition in knowledge and insight and i know that i am pure i know that these things are pure within me clean and stainless not as to them do my disciples protect me nor do i expect this of my disciples as the end of the sutta
commentary: this reveals a few things firstly that you see this is the manga the disciple of the buddha with the greatest psychic power and yet here you can see that the deva his former attendant former monk attendant i think came to visit him and told him that the devadatta is planning that he wants to lead the sangha of monks now this is interesting because you can see from here and other sutras that the arahants including the buddha they generally spend their time in meditation even though they had psychic power they didn't go around trying to be inquisitive and find out what this guy is thinking what that fellow is thinking what this fellow is planning and all that because if he if he did if they did that then they'll be very busy and they would be still having attachment to what's going on in the world and it's because the buddha and his disciples they are enlightened and they see this world as appearing only in consciousness a bit like a dream that is why they are not so concerned with what's going on in the world and the other thing that's interesting here is that you see this devadatta is 1 of the disciples 1 of the well-known disciples of the buddha and also a relation of the buddha and he had according to the vinaya he had obtained all the 8 psychic powers ah sorry all the 8 janas and the 4 rupa janas and the 4 aru pajanas and because of that he had psychic power and he had great psychic power but because he still had this greed a very strong greed to his ego was still very strong he wanted to take the place of the buddha so because of that now he was planning how to take the the the buddha's place in fact in our vinaya books we find that 1 day he approached the buddha and told the buddha now you are old why don't you step down let me take over and lead the sangha and he said that 3 times in our binary books and then the buddha scolded him sort of scolded him in front of the sangha saying that he's like like a spit something that you spit away and yet you think you want to lead the sangha from there the hatred for the buddha increased and then he planned to kill the buddha so here the deva is telling maha mogalana that because of this great greedy thought to lead the sangha his psychic power immediately declined and the buddha was saying watch your words mughlana what's your words but even now the foolish fellow will betray himself that means the the buddha is saying your words what you are saying because will show his true colors very soon [Music] then the buddha talked about the 5 types of impure teachers and this is quite interesting that there are teachers who are impure and they try to hide the fragment and the disciples also try to protect the teacher because um this is concerned with the personality culture some people the hero worship a teacher and because of that they think that teacher is famous and so because if they are a disciple of a famous teacher they are also they also like that not great the teacher is great they are also great so because of that now they want to protect the image of their teacher you see and in that way they are indirectly protecting them their own reputation and it's clear in the buddha's teachings and we don't take refuge with any with anybody and we only take refuge with the buddha dharma and sangha we always remember we take the refuge with the buddha dharma and sangha and whatever a teacher tells us we have to compare it with the buddha's discourses whether it's consistent with the buddha's discourses it is consistently accepted it is not consistent the buddha says in the angutronic ayah then we don't accept it because nowadays there's a lot of personality cults around now we have to be cautious next 2 times
